description:
You are acting as a database reliability engineer who is tidying up the PostgreSQL-dump
    backup area.  
    The work folder layout is already present on the machine:

      • /home/user/db_archives/          ← permanent, read-only dump storage  
      • /home/user/db_backups/           ← short-term working area (writeable)  

    Inside /home/user/db_archives/ there are four compressed SQL dump files:

      prod_20230918.sql.gz
      prod_20230919.sql.gz
      stg_20230918.sql.gz
      stg_20230919.sql.gz

    Inside /home/user/db_backups/ there are two *out-of-date* symbolic links
    that still point at the “20230918” files:

      latest_prod      → /home/user/db_archives/prod_20230918.sql.gz
      latest_staging   → /home/user/db_archives/stg_20230918.sql.gz

    Your assignment:

    1. Remove the two obsolete symbolic links in /home/user/db_backups/.
    2. Create two fresh symbolic links in the same directory that point to the *_20230919*
       dumps located in /home/user/db_archives/:
         • /home/user/db_backups/latest_prod      → /home/user/db_archives/prod_20230919.sql.gz
         • /home/user/db_backups/latest_staging   → /home/user/db_archives/stg_20230919.sql.gz
       The links must be *symbolic* (not hard links) and must use absolute paths exactly
       as shown above.
    3. Write (or overwrite) a plain-text log file called
         /home/user/db_backups/update_symlinks.log
       The file *must* contain exactly the two lines below, **nothing else, no extra
       whitespace or newline at the end of the file**:

latest_prod -> /home/user/db_archives/prod_20230919.sql.gz
latest_staging -> /home/user/db_archives/stg_20230919.sql.gz

    Notes & verification rules:

      • The automated tests will confirm that:
          – The two symbolic links exist at the exact paths given.
          – Each link resolves to the exact target file listed above.
          – The targets themselves remain untouched.
          – The log file exists, is a regular file, has the exact two lines
            (no trailing blank line), and is timestamp-free.
      • You do not need root privileges.
      • Only standard GNU/Linux user-space tools are required.
      • Aim to complete the task in three to four terminal commands.
truth:
Initial file system (before agent starts):

      Directory /home/user/db_archives/ contains:
        - prod_20230918.sql.gz          (empty placeholder file, size 0 B)
        - prod_20230919.sql.gz          (empty placeholder file, size 0 B)
        - stg_20230918.sql.gz           (empty placeholder file, size 0 B)
        - stg_20230919.sql.gz           (empty placeholder file, size 0 B)

      Directory /home/user/db_backups/ contains:
        • symbolic link latest_prod      → /home/user/db_archives/prod_20230918.sql.gz
        • symbolic link latest_staging   → /home/user/db_archives/stg_20230918.sql.gz
        • (no update_symlinks.log file yet)

    Expected final state (after agent completes):

      /home/user/db_backups/latest_prod
        – type: symbolic link
        – points **exactly** to /home/user/db_archives/prod_20230919.sql.gz

      /home/user/db_backups/latest_staging
        – type: symbolic link
        – points **exactly** to /home/user/db_archives/stg_20230919.sql.gz

      /home/user/db_backups/update_symlinks.log
        – type: regular file, mode 0644 or stricter
        – contents (8-bit clean, Unix line endings, NO trailing newline):

latest_prod -> /home/user/db_archives/prod_20230919.sql.gz
latest_staging -> /home/user/db_archives/stg_20230919.sql.gz

    Any resident file not mentioned above must remain unchanged.
